mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity present value and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is greater than -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 3.5 percent signals deteriorating household income expectations and falling discretionary expenditure, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 3.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of an unemployment rate above 3.5 percent and retail sales below 700000 million dollars captures a consumer-labor feedback loop that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through downward earnings revision.
failure_condition: A linear model with the UNRATE-RSAFS interaction term does not significantly outperform a main-effects-only model in predicting 20-day returns (F-test p-value greater than 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in industrial production signals an inventory correction that reduces manufacturing earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of late-cycle instances following INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 300 signals elevated inflation that sustains restrictive monetary policy, raising real interest rates and equity risk premiums to produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of housing starts lead residential construction activity and related durable goods demand, lifting cyclical corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between HOUST level and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between an elevated federal funds rate above 4.0 percent and declining payroll employment signals restrictive monetary policy hitting the real economy, amplifying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days beyond the independent effects of each variable.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and PAYEMS below prior 3-month level
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS changes does not produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock demand collapse, a contraction in real GDP produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as aggregate demand shortfalls drive broad-based earnings misses.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of post-shock quarters when GDPC1 contracts.
